verificationNote: Indicative requirements for ISO 27001:2022 organizational controls. Not verbatim ISO text.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a set of information security policies approved by management, communicated to all relevant parties, and reviewed at planned intervals.
Define, allocate, and communicate all information security roles and responsibilities across the organization.
Identify and segregate conflicting duties and areas of responsibility, implementing compensating controls where full segregation is impractical.
Collect, analyse, and produce actionable threat intelligence relevant to the organization's risk profile.
Develop and maintain an inventory of information and associated assets, assigning ownership for each asset.
Classify information according to confidentiality, integrity, and availability needs, with documented classification criteria.
Establish access control rules based on business and security requirements, applying least privilege and need-to-know principles.
Manage the full lifecycle of identities including provisioning, verification, maintenance, and timely deactivation.
Define and implement processes to manage information security risks in supplier relationships, including contractual security requirements.
Establish processes for secure acquisition, use, management, and exit from cloud services, addressing shared responsibility.
Plan and prepare for information security incident management with defined roles, procedures, and classification schemes.
Respond to information security incidents following documented procedures including containment, eradication, and recovery.
Plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during organizational disruptions.
Identify, document, and maintain currency of all legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements relevant to information security.
Identify and meet requirements for privacy and protection of PII as required by applicable laws, regulations, and contractual obligations.
Conduct independent reviews of the ISMS approach and implementation at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.
